name: Trout Sauteed in Butter
recipeInstructions:
Skillet-size brook, brown, and rainbow trout are never more tasty than when prepared this way. This happy marriage of fresh trout and butter yields a delicious dish.
For best results:
1. Cook fish slowly to keep the flesh moist but with a golden brown exterior.
2. Avoid breaking the trout into pieces by handling gently.
3. Use flour, not cornmeal, to coat the trout since it has delicate flavor.
4. Season the trout with salt and pepper before coating in flour.
5. Melt butter in a large iron skillet over medium heat until hot but not colored.
6. Gently place the seasoned trout into the skillet.
7. Maintain a steady, low heat for 15-20 minutes, depending on trout size.
8. As soon as the flour coating sets on the bottom, carefully remove each trout using a gentle shake of the skillet to prevent overcooking.
9. After 10 minutes of slow browning, flip the trout and repeat the process.
10. Serve the trout with remaining butter melted in the skillet, lemon juice added, and garnished with parsley sprigs and lemon wedges.
